title: AI ROI Calculator for Operations Teams
subtitle: Stop guessing whether automation is worth it. Build a business case with real numbers.

inputs.label: People Involved per Unit
inputs.hint: Count every handoff across the workflow

inputs.label: Time per Unit
inputs.hint: Sum of all human touches, not one person's time

inputs.label: Fully Loaded Hourly Cost
inputs.hint: If unknown, use 1.3x base hourly rate

inputs.label: Current Error Rate
inputs.hint: Be honest, most teams undercount this

inputs.label: Cost per Error
inputs.hint: Include rework time, customer impact, refunds

inputs.label: Automation Build Cost
inputs.hint: One-time vendor or internal build estimate

outputs.label: Payback Period
outputs.formula: build_cost / Math.max(1, ((((volume_per_month * time_per_unit * people_involved) / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.80) - monthly_maintenance))

outputs.label: First Year ROI
outputs.formula: ((((((volume_per_month * time_per_unit * people_involved) / 60) * hourly_cost * 12 * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 12 * 0.80) - (monthly_maintenance * 12)) - build_cost) / build_cost) * 100

recommendations.when: (build_cost / Math.max(1, ((((volume_per_month * time_per_unit * people_involved) / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.80) - monthly_maintenance))) < 6
recommendations.text: Green light. Payback under 6 months means this workflow is a priority build. Lock in a vendor or your internal team this quarter before the opportunity cost compounds.

recommendations.when: (build_cost / Math.max(1, ((((volume_per_month * time_per_unit * people_involved) / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.80) - monthly_maintenance))) >= 6 && (build_cost / Math.max(1, ((((volume_per_month * time_per_unit * people_involved) / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.80) - monthly_maintenance))) <= 18
recommendations.text: Worth building, but sharpen the numbers first. Validate your error rate and time per unit with a one-week audit so leadership cannot pick apart the business case.

recommendations.when: (build_cost / Math.max(1, ((((volume_per_month * time_per_unit * people_involved) / 60) * hourly_cost * 0.75) + (volume_per_month * (error_rate / 100) * cost_per_error * 0.80) - monthly_maintenance))) > 18
recommendations.text: Shelf this one and pick a higher volume or higher error workflow. Payback over 18 months usually means the process is too small or the build scope is too large.
Your error rate is doing a lot of the lifting in this ROI. Automating this workflow is really a quality play, frame the business case around customer impact and compliance, not just labor savings.
Volume is low for a custom build. Consider a lightweight no code approach with n8n or Zapier first to keep build cost under $1,500 and protect your payback window.
Four plus handoffs per unit is a coordination tax, not a task problem. Map the workflow end to end before automating so you do not just speed up a broken process.

capture_cta.headline: Want me to pressure test your numbers?
capture_cta.description: Send me your calculator results and I will tell you which workflow to automate first, what it should cost to build, and where the ROI is hiding in your ops data. No pitch, just a straight read.
